entry_model: Scanner TRUE is used only for entry candidate logic. It is not used as an exit signal.
exit_model: Exits use price stops or max holding only. Price stop uses intraday low; gap below stop exits at open.

summary.description: Entry: Enter next open after the scanner signal first turns TRUE. Exit: Initial stop is entry-signal-day Low10. Stop only moves up when the latest Low10 rises.
Entry: Enter next open after the scanner signal first turns TRUE. Exit: Initial stop is entry-signal-day Low20. This matches the current Suggested Stop idea.
Entry: Enter next open after the scanner signal first turns TRUE. Exit: Low20 with a half-ATR buffer, intended to reduce small whipsaw stop-outs.
Entry: Enter next open after the scanner signal first turns TRUE. Exit: HHV20 minus 2.5 x ATR20, trailed upward only.
Entry: Enter next open after the scanner signal first turns TRUE. Exit: Uses MA50 as the price stop, trailed upward only. This is slower but often less noisy.
Entry: Enter next open after the scanner signal first turns TRUE. Exit: No price stop. Exit only at max holding days. Included as a loose benchmark.
Entry: Enter next open after the scanner signal stays TRUE for 2 consecutive trading days. Exit: Initial stop is entry-signal-day Low10. Stop only moves up when the latest Low10 rises.
Entry: Enter next open after the scanner signal stays TRUE for 2 consecutive trading days. Exit: Initial stop is entry-signal-day Low20. This matches the current Suggested Stop idea.

Entry: Enter next open after the scanner signal stays TRUE for 2 consecutive trading days. Exit: Uses MA50 as the price stop, trailed upward only. This is slower but often less noisy.
Entry: Enter next open after the scanner signal stays TRUE for 2 consecutive trading days. Exit: No price stop. Exit only at max holding days. Included as a loose benchmark.
Entry: Enter next open after the scanner signal stays TRUE for 3 consecutive trading days. Exit: Initial stop is entry-signal-day Low10. Stop only moves up when the latest Low10 rises.

Entry: Enter next open after the scanner signal stays TRUE for 3 consecutive trading days. Exit: Uses MA50 as the price stop, trailed upward only. This is slower but often less noisy.
Entry: Enter next open after the scanner signal stays TRUE for 3 consecutive trading days. Exit: No price stop. Exit only at max holding days. Included as a loose benchmark.
Entry: After a first signal, enter next open if close breaks the prior 5-day high within 5 trading days. Exit: Initial stop is entry-signal-day Low10. Stop only moves up when the latest Low10 rises.
Entry: After a first signal, enter next open if close breaks the prior 5-day high within 5 trading days. Exit: Initial stop is entry-signal-day Low20. This matches the current Suggested Stop idea.

Entry: After a first signal, enter next open if close breaks the prior 5-day high within 5 trading days. Exit: Uses MA50 as the price stop, trailed upward only. This is slower but often less noisy.
Entry: After a first signal, enter next open if close breaks the prior 5-day high within 5 trading days. Exit: No price stop. Exit only at max holding days. Included as a loose benchmark.
Entry: First signal plus top-30% score, ER63 >= 0.25, and R63 >= 0.05. Exit: Initial stop is entry-signal-day Low10. Stop only moves up when the latest Low10 rises.
Entry: First signal plus top-30% score, ER63 >= 0.25, and R63 >= 0.05. Exit: Initial stop is entry-signal-day Low20. This matches the current Suggested Stop idea.
